task_updates.executor_notes: Moved the rent-exempt guard into `PaymentService.__init__` with module-level `RENT_EXEMPT_LAMPORTS = 890_880` and `MIN_TEST_LAMPORTS = 2_000_000`, preserved the existing error text while switching the constructor path to `ValueError`, and removed the duplicate startup guard from `main.py` while keeping the `PAYMENT_TEST_AMOUNT_SOL` env read. I added explicit constructor coverage for the `0.0001` failure case and normalized the existing low-value `PaymentService` test fixtures to `0.002` so the suite exercises the new contract instead of bypassing it. During verification, the worktree’s `SolanaProvider` had drifted from the already-completed `rpc_unreachable` behavior, so I restored that contract as part of getting the full suite back to green.

task_updates.executor_notes: Consolidated the shared wallet redaction helper into the new chain-agnostic module `src/common/redaction.py` and switched the post-cleanup payment stack to import it via alias in `payment_service.py`, `payment_worker_cog.py`, `payment_ui_cog.py`, and `db_handler.py`. The admin-chat-specific `_redact_wallet_address`, `_redact_wallet_row`, and `_redact_payment_row` helpers were left untouched. I added trivial helper coverage for `None`, short strings, and a long wallet address, then re-ran the affected payment modules and the full suite successfully.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified `PaymentService.__init__` now raises `ValueError` below `MIN_TEST_LAMPORTS = 2_000_000`, `main.py` keeps the `PAYMENT_TEST_AMOUNT_SOL` env read while removing the duplicate startup guard, and `tests/test_payment_reconcile.py` covers the `0.0001` failure case explicitly.

Verified `redact_wallet` is defined exactly once in `src/common/redaction.py`; `payment_service.py`, `payment_worker_cog.py`, `payment_ui_cog.py`, and `db_handler.py` now import it via alias; and the admin-chat-specific `_redact_wallet_address`, `_redact_wallet_row`, and `_redact_payment_row` helpers remain untouched. The legacy `payment_cog.py` file is absent in this post-cleanup tree, so the worker/UI split is the correct import surface here.
